findings.title: #211 queueName override is silently ignored for the common (object-with-arn) event shapes
findings.detail: resolveQueueName (sqs.js:31-34) correctly produces `{...rawDef, queueName: override}` and is wired into _create (sqs.js:65). But the resulting def is then passed to SQSEventDefinition, whose switch evaluates `typeof rawSqsEventDefinition.arn === 'string'` BEFORE `typeof rawSqsEventDefinition.queueName === 'string'` (sqs-event-definition.js:19-28). For any event definition that carries an `arn` (the realistic case), queueName is derived from the ARN and the override is discarded. I reproduced this against every documented event form in README.md:59-77: form2 `{arn: '...'}`, form3 `{queueName, arn: Fn::GetAtt}`, and forms 4/5 `{arn: Fn::GetAtt}` (all resolve to a string `arn` via _resolveFn). In each case `effective queueName` stays the event's queue, override applied = false. Only the bare string form (`sqs: arn:...`) actually overrides. So the feature is half-wired: the pure helper works in isolation and the unit test at test/index.js:176-181 only exercises a def with `queueName` and NO `arn`, giving false confidence. README.md:114 explicitly promises it 'overrides the queue name resolved from each sqs event definition', which is not true for 4 of the 5 documented shapes.
findings.recommendation: Make the override authoritative regardless of how queueName is otherwise derived. Either (a) strip `arn` when applying the override (`{...omit(['arn'], rawDef), queueName: override}`) so the switch falls through to the queueName branch, or (b) move queueName resolution into SQSEventDefinition so an explicit queueName wins over arn, or (c) apply the override AFTER SQSEventDefinition is built (set sqsEvent.queueName and recompute arn). Add a regression test for an `{arn: 'arn:...'}` def with an override set.

findings.title: #211 resolveQueueName spreads a string ARN into a char-indexed object
findings.detail: When the event definition is a string ARN (README form 1: `- sqs: arn:aws:sqs:...`) and an override is set, `{...rawSqsEventDefinition, queueName: options.queueName}` spreads the string character-by-character into `{0:'a',1:'r',2:'n',...,queueName:'override'}`. It happens to work for queueName resolution only because SQSEventDefinition's switch hits the `.queueName` branch (the spread object's `.arn` is undefined), but the def object handed downstream is malformed and Object.assign(this, omit(['arn','queueName','enabled'], def)) at sqs-event-definition.js:38 then copies all the numeric character keys onto the event definition instance. The test at test/index.js:127-131 only asserts `resolved.queueName` so it masks this. Note this is the only path where the override currently 'works', which makes the malformation easy to overlook.
findings.recommendation: Guard the spread for non-object inputs, e.g. `const base = typeof rawDef === 'string' ? {arn: rawDef} : rawDef;` then apply the override (and per the related finding, strip arn so the override actually wins). This also yields a clean def object instead of a char-indexed one.

findings.title: #100 final-attempt failure is logged but the give-up has no distinct signal
findings.detail: The retry is correctly bounded (default maximumRetryAttempts=10 -> exactly 10 total attempts, verified; no infinite loop) and each failed attempt is logged with `attempt N/M` (kinesis.js:100-103). However, on the last attempt it logs the same per-attempt warning and then `shouldRetry(0)` returns false, so the record is dropped with no explicit 'giving up after N attempts' message. Operationally a reader sees 'attempt 10/10 failed' and then silence, with no clear indication the message was abandoned. This is a polish issue, not a correctness bug; the feature meets the bounded+logged acceptance criterion.
findings.recommendation: Optional: emit a distinct `this.log.error('Kinesis handler for ${functionKey} exhausted ${maximumRetryAttempts} attempts; dropping batch')` after the loop gives up, mirroring how operators expect a terminal log line.

findings.title: SQS index.js retains the redundant null pre-init block — inconsistent with the other 3 plugin classes and a deviation from its own plan doc
findings.detail: The v4 migration was supposed to apply the same plugin-class shape to all 4 packages. Each plan doc step 2 (01/02/03/04) explicitly says "Constructor 3rd arg {log} = {} ... drop redundant this.x = null;". s3/index.js (lines 18-30), kinesis/index.js (17-29) and dynamodb-streams/index.js (17-29) all dropped the null pre-assignments. Only ServerlessOfflineSQS kept them: lines 35-39 still do `this.cliOptions = null; this.options = null; this.sqs = null; this.lambda = null; this.serverless = null;` immediately before re-assigning the real values at 41-43. The staged diff confirms this block was carried over from the original file rather than removed. Net effect: the four plugin index classes are no longer uniform (the lens explicitly asks for the v4 migration to be applied uniformly with the same constructor shape), and SQS deviates from 02-package-sqs.md step 2 without any plan-doc justification. NOTE: this applies ONLY to the plugin class; the driver classes (sqs.js/s3.js keep 3 null-inits, kinesis.js/dynamodb-streams.js keep 2) correctly retain declare-null-then-assign per conventions.md §3, and those are uniform — do not touch the drivers.
findings.recommendation: Delete lines 35-39 of packages/serverless-offline-sqs/src/index.js so the constructor matches s3/kinesis/ddb (assign cliOptions, serverless, log directly). This is the only change needed to make all 4 plugin classes byte-uniform in shape.

The kinesis bounded-retry helper `shouldRetry` (kinesis.js:15-17) is the #100 (dolsem) re-implementation but its comment only says "Bounded retry (mirrors dynamodb-streams)" with no `// #100 (dolsem)` credit, even though 03-package-kinesis.md step 9 and 00-goal.md both attribute it to dolsem and the goal requires author credits. findings.title: Pre-existing copy-paste arg name `rawSqsEventDefinition` in dynamodb-streams-event-definition.js not cleaned up while the package was being touched
findings.detail: conventions.md §6 mandates the un-normalized config arg be named `raw<Service>EventDefinition`. kinesis-event-definition.js correctly uses `rawKinesisEventDefinition`, and the ddb driver dynamodb-streams.js:52 correctly uses `rawDynamodbStreamsEventDefinition`. But dynamodb-streams-event-definition.js:10 names its constructor parameter `rawSqsEventDefinition` (a leftover copy-paste from SQS), referenced again at lines 19, 24, 29, 42. This file was NOT staged in the PR (the naming bug is pre-existing, not introduced), so it is not a regression — but the 04 plan had the agent renaming the sibling KinesisEvent->DynamodbStreamsEvent copy-paste for exactly this clarity reason, and this matching copy-paste in the same package was left as-is. Flagging as an inconsistency the PR could have closed cheaply, not a defect introduced.

findings.title: #249 await this.lambda.create applied only to kinesis (inconsistent, but not a regression)
findings.detail: kinesis/src/index.js:104 was changed to `await this.lambda.create(lambdas)` while s3 (105), sqs (124), and dynamodb-streams (104) still call `this.lambda.create(lambdas)` without await. I verified serverless-offline's Lambda.create is synchronous and returns undefined (node_modules/serverless-offline/src/lambda/Lambda.js:25-29 — a plain forEach), so awaiting undefined is a no-op. This is NOT a behavior break in the installed version; it only future-proofs kinesis if create ever becomes async. Flagging only the inconsistency.
findings.recommendation: Optional: for consistency and the same future-proofing rationale, add `await` to the lambda.create calls in s3/sqs/dynamodb-streams as well, or drop it from kinesis. No functional impact either way today.

findings.detail: The require of './s3-event-definition' was removed from s3.js and the _create/_s3Event methods deleted (all genuinely dead — verified no remaining references in packages/serverless-offline-s3/src except the file itself). However the file packages/serverless-offline-s3/src/s3-event-definition.js still exists and is still listed in packages/serverless-offline-s3/package.json `files`. It is now unimported dead code. This is harmless (it just ships an unused module) and not a regression, but it is leftover cleanup. Note kinesis/sqs/dynamodb-streams still legitimately use their *-event-definition.js files.

findings.title: Kinesis #omit-fix "regression" tests are tautological — they pass under the original bug
findings.detail: Both tests labelled "omit-fix regression" claim to guard the fix that changed omit(['arn','tableName','enabled']) -> omit(['arn','streamName','enabled']) in kinesis-event-definition.js:43. I reproduced the OLD buggy omit list and ran both test inputs through it: both still pass. Test 1 (line 112) passes {streamName:'polls', batchSize:50,...}: under the bug, omit keeps streamName but Object.assign just re-writes this.streamName with the identical resolved value 'polls', so streamName/arn/batchSize assertions all hold. Test 2 (line 139) uses the {arn} form with no conflicting streamName prop, so streamName is never in the input to leak. The bug only manifests when a raw streamName prop differs from the resolved streamName (e.g. {arn:'...stream/polls', streamName:'different'}), which NEITHER test exercises. The tests therefore cannot fail if the bug is reintroduced.
findings.recommendation: Add a case where the raw streamName disagrees with the resolved one, e.g. new KinesisEventDefinition({arn:'arn:aws:kinesis:eu-west-1:0:stream/polls', streamName:'attacker'}, ...) and assert def.streamName === 'polls' (resolved from ARN wins, raw streamName is omitted). Under the old omit(['arn','tableName','enabled']) this returns 'attacker' and fails; under the fix it returns 'polls'.

findings.title: SQS and Kinesis #166 guards only test the mapper, not the call site that actually had the bug
findings.detail: #166 was a CALL-SITE bug: sqs.js/kinesis.js passed this.region (undefined on the instance) instead of this.options.region into the event mapper. The guard tests construct new SQSEvent([...], 'eu-west-1', arn) and new KinesisEvent(chunk, 'us-east-2', ...) with an explicit region string, so they only verify the mapper copies its region argument to awsRegion. I confirmed new SQSEvent([...], undefined, arn) yields awsRegion=undefined — the mapper faithfully forwards whatever it is given. Reverting sqs.js:130 / kinesis.js:95 back to this.region would NOT fail either test. The DDB package correctly adds a source-text call-site guard (dynamodb-streams/test/index.js:189-194 checks this.options.region present and rejects /new DynamodbStreamsEvent\(chunk, this\.region\b/), but SQS and Kinesis have no equivalent — grep for this.options.region / readFileSync in those two test files returns nothing.
findings.recommendation: Mirror the DDB call-site guard in the SQS and Kinesis suites: readFileSync('src/sqs.js') and assert it includes 'this.options.region' and does NOT match /new SQSEvent\(messages, this\.region\b/; likewise for kinesis.js / new KinesisEvent(chunk, this.region. This is the only assertion that actually fails on a #166 reversion.

findings.title: SQS "80-char limit" test is tautological given the index-based implementation
findings.detail: The test feeds a 200-char MessageId and asserts Id.length <= 80. But toDeleteEntries (sqs.js:27) computes Id = String(index), so the MessageId is never read; I confirmed it returns Id='0' (length 1). The assertion holds for any input and provides no signal — it cannot catch a regression to the old MessageId-based Id (the actual #253 bug) because the regression-relevant input (a long MessageId) is ignored by the new code by design. The genuinely meaningful #253 test is the uniqueness one at line 72-76, which is real (it would fail if Id reverted to MessageId since all MessageIds are 'same').
findings.recommendation: Either drop this test as non-informative, or convert it into a real #253 guard by asserting both properties on a colliding+long batch in one shot and documenting that uniqueness (not length) is the load-bearing invariant. Length is structurally guaranteed by String(index).

findings.title: Kinesis #100 bounded-retry coverage tests a re-implemented loop, not the production recursion
findings.detail: shouldRetry (kinesis.js:17) is just remainingAttempts > 0; the tests at 161-169 are fine but trivial. The "caps the loop" test (171-180) re-implements the decrement-and-recurse loop inside the test body (run = (remaining,inv) => shouldRetry(remaining) ? run(remaining-1, inv+1) : inv) and asserts it terminates after maximumRetryAttempts iterations. This verifies the test's own re-implementation, not the actual task() recursion in kinesis.js:91-114 or its error-logging side effect (#100 also requires logging each failed attempt — the attempt-counter log at line 101-103 is entirely untested). Removing the shouldRetry guard from the real task() would not fail any test.
findings.recommendation: Extract the retry/log behaviour into a testable pure-ish unit (e.g. a runWithRetry(fn, attempts, log) helper) and assert (a) fn is invoked exactly maximumRetryAttempts times on persistent failure and (b) log.warning is called per attempt with the attempt counter. Otherwise the #100 logging requirement has zero coverage.

findings.title: No test covers the #253 chunk(10, ...) interaction (unique Ids must be unique only within each 10-entry deleteMessageBatch call)
findings.detail: AWS requires deleteMessageBatch entry Ids unique within a single batch of <=10. The implementation chunks toDeleteEntries(messages) by 10 (sqs.js:136) AFTER assigning global indices 0..N, so Ids stay unique across the whole batch — which is correct. The uniqueness test (25 messages) confirms global uniqueness, which is sufficient, but no test asserts the chunk size (<=10 per call) constraint that is the actual SQS API limit motivating #253. If someone reverted the index-based Id to a fixed per-chunk index (0..9 repeated per chunk), global uniqueness would break but the existing 25-element test already catches that. So this is covered indirectly; the gap is only that the 10-per-call chunking itself is untested.
findings.recommendation: Optional: add a small assertion that exercises chunking, or document that toDeleteEntries owning a global monotonic index is what guarantees per-chunk uniqueness. Low priority since the 25-element uniqueness test transitively guards the regression.

findings.title: resolveQueueName string-ARN override path is asserted loosely and ignores the spread-of-string artifact
findings.detail: When given a string ARN def and an override, resolveQueueName returns {...stringArn, queueName:'override'} (sqs.js:33). Spreading a string yields numeric-index character keys plus queueName. The test only asserts resolved.queueName === 'override' and does not verify that the resulting object is consumable downstream by SQSEventDefinition (whose switch keys on typeof rawSqsEventDefinition.queueName, so it happens to work) nor that the numeric-key pollution is harmless. The behaviour is correct in practice but the test gives false confidence that the string-ARN+override combination produces a clean definition.
findings.recommendation: Either assert the downstream result (new SQSEventDefinition(resolveQueueName({queueName:'override'}, 'arn:...:fromEvent'), region, acct).queueName === 'override' and .arn ends in :override), or document that the spread-of-string keys are inert. The existing line-176 test already does the clean object+override path; extending it to the string-ARN path would close the gap.

findings.title: normalizeLog null-safety is well covered; awsRegion non-undefined assertions are real (positive note)
findings.detail: Reviewed for completeness: normalizeLog tests across all four suites correctly cover null/undefined (no throw), key-set equality with defaultLog, overlay precedence, default-debug-is-noop, and non-mutation of the injected logger. These are genuine, not tautological. The #98 assertStreamEnabled tests (ddb 105-124) are strong: they assert exact error message text for undefined/null/'' and the happy path returns the arn — a reversion that drops the guard would fail. No emulator class (new SQS/Kinesis/DynamodbStreams/S3) is constructed in any test, so no real AWS/Minio client is built (the AWS-SDK-v2 maintenance warning during the SQS/Kinesis runs is only a require() side-effect of aws-sdk/clients/* at module top, not a constructed client) — the docker-free goal is met.
findings.recommendation: No action required; recorded so the strong areas are not mistaken for gaps. If desired, consider lazy-requiring aws-sdk inside the SQS/Kinesis constructors so importing the pure exports (toDeleteEntries/resolveQueueName/shouldRetry) for tests does not trigger the SDK load at all.

tool_input.rationale:
Verified the code claims are all accurate, but the finding itself is correctly labeled a nit with no functional impact and is not worth fixing.

Confirmed facts:

- The other three plugins call it without await: packages/serverless-offline-s3/src/index.js:105, packages/serverless-offline-sqs/src/index.js:124, packages/serverless-offline-dynamodb-streams/src/index.js:104.

Why real=false:
1. No behavior change today in the installed version (await on undefined resolves immediately).
2. Not a regression — kinesis behaves identically before/after; the other three were never awaited and remain unchanged.
3. It is a pure stylistic inconsistency. The finding's own proposed fix is explicitly optional ("No functional impact either way today").
4. The PR's #249 acceptance criterion ("kinesis await create") is satisfied; adding await elsewhere or removing it from kinesis is scope expansion, not a fix for a defect.

This is at most a nit, and per the instructions cosmetic/uncertain items default to real=false.

tool_input.rationale:
Verified against the code. The finding's facts are all correct: at packages/serverless-offline-sqs/src/sqs.js:26-27, `toDeleteEntries` computes `Id: String(index)` and never destructures/reads `MessageId` (only `ReceiptHandle`). I confirmed empirically that the test input (a 200-char MessageId) yields `Id = "0"` (length 1), so the assertion `Id.length <= 80` at test/index.js:78-83 is tautological — `String(index)` makes length structurally guaranteed for any realistic batch, and the input that would matter (a long MessageId) is ignored by the new implementation by design. The assertion therefore provides no regression signal and could not catch a revert to MessageId-based Ids.

However, this is a test-hygiene nit, not an issue worth fixing in this PR: (1) the test is not incorrect — it asserts a true property and passes; (2) the genuinely load-bearing #253 invariant (uniqueness when MessageIds collide) is ALREADY covered by the real test at test/index.js:72-76, which uses 25 colliding 'same' MessageIds and asserts `new Set(ids).size === ids.length` — that test WOULD fail on a regression to MessageId-based Ids. So there is no correctness defect and no coverage gap; the only downside is one redundant/non-informative test. That is cosmetic and does not block or meaningfully degrade the PR.

tool_input.content:
## What & why

Serverless Framework **v4 removed** `@serverless/utils/log` and `serverless.cli.log`. Our four
`serverless-offline-*` plugins `require` that module at load time, so they **crash on every `sls`
command under v4** (`sls package`, `sls offline`, …). This PR migrates all four to the logger that
the framework **injects as the plugin constructor's 3rd argument** (`{log}`) — which has existed
since serverless **v3.0**, so the change is **backward compatible with v3**.

While we were in here owning this code, we also re-implemented the worthwhile ideas from several
long-open community PRs as our own, tested code, and fixed a batch of latent bugs the migration
surfaced.

> Supersedes the partial v4 PR #264 (s3+sqs only): this covers **all four** plugins, keeps peers
> **wide** (no forced v14 cutover), and adds the unit tests + correctness fixes that one was missing.
> See `docs/serverless-v4-migration-review.md` and `docs/open-pr-triage.md` for the full analysis.

## Changes per package

**All four** — remove the dead `@serverless/utils/log` require; take `{log}` from the 3rd ctor arg via a
new `src/log.js` shim (full level set, console fallback); thread it into the emulator; replace
`serverless.cli.log` → `this.log.notice`. Minor version bump; peer kept `^10.0.2 || >=11`.

- **serverless-offline-s3** (7.1.0) — fix `log.warn`→`log.warning`; delete dead `_create`/`_s3Event`
  methods and the now-orphaned `s3-event-definition.js`.
- **serverless-offline-sqs** (8.1.0) — fix `this.region`→`this.options.region` so events carry the
  right `awsRegion` (**#166**, thanks @zlalvani); unique `deleteMessageBatch` entry Ids within a batch
  (**#253**, thanks @flipscholtz); `custom.serverless-offline-sqs.queueName` override (**#211**, thanks
  @mfamilia).
- **serverless-offline-kinesis** (7.1.0) — `awsRegion` fix (**#166**); replace the infinite *silent*
  handler retry with a **bounded, logged** retry (default `maximumRetryAttempts: 10`, mirrors
  dynamodb-streams) (**#100**, thanks @dolsem); `await lambda.create(...)` (**#249**, thanks @gabsong);
  fix `kinesis-event-definition` omit list (`tableName`→`streamName` copy-paste bug); now logs handler errors.
- **serverless-offline-dynamodb-streams** (7.1.0) — `awsRegion` fix (**#166**); clear
  `Table <name> does not have streams enabled` error (**#98**, thanks @dolsem); rename the
  copy-pasted `KinesisEvent` class to `DynamodbStreamsEvent`; now logs handler errors.

## Compatibility

- Works on **serverless v3 and v4** (the `{log}` 3rd arg exists in both; console fallback if absent).
- Peer ranges kept **wide** (`serverless-offline: ^10.0.2 || >=11`) — services can migrate one at a time.
- **Minor** version bumps (behavior-preserving + additive). No new runtime dependencies.

## Testing

- ✅ `npm run eslint` (lint + Prettier) clean across the repo.
- ✅ **71 new docker-free AVA unit tests** — the offline plugins previously had **zero** unit tests.
  Covers the log shim, the event mappers (with `awsRegion` regression guards for #166), the
  delete-batch Id uniqueness (#253), the non-mutating queueName override incl. arn-bearing shapes
  (#211), the kinesis omit-fix (discriminating test), and source-level call-site guards.
- ⚠️ **Not run here** (documented, follow-up): the Docker integration suite
  (`tests/serverless-plugins-integration`, needs minio/elasticmq/kinesalite/dynamodb-local) and v4
  end-to-end (`sls package`/`offline` under `serverless@4`, needs `SERVERLESS_ACCESS_KEY`). The
  recommended CI v4 matrix is described in `docs/testing-and-ci.md`.

## Tooling added

Repo docs under `docs/` (architecture, conventions, testing-and-ci, publishing-and-versioning, the
review + triage, and per-task plans), plus a `serverless-plugin-dev` skill and a
`serverless-plugin-author` agent under `.claude/` to help future work on these plugins.

## Credits

Re-implements ideas from community PRs **#166** (@zlalvani), **#253** (@flipscholtz), **#211**
(@mfamilia), **#98** & **#100** (@dolsem), **#249** (@gabsong) — thank you. 🙏

## Follow-ups (not in this PR)

- CI v4 build matrix (serverless 3 *and* 4).
- **#183** DLQ autocreate from `RedrivePolicy` and **#168** host/start — deferred until validated
  against the Docker + v4 integration lane (see `docs/plans/07-deferred-and-closed-prs.md`).
- Publish (canary → stable via lerna), then bump consuming services (CLXP-103).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
</content>
